revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:御霊神社 (奈良市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Goryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:20160801 Goryo-jinja.jpg |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}{{ill|Yakushidō-chō|ja|薬師堂町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushidō-chō|1=Yakushidōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28685937}} 24 |enshrined deity = {{ill|Princess Inoe|en|Princess Inoe|qid=Q7244649|lt=Empress Inoue|1=Princess Inoe}}
{{ill|Prince Osabe|ja|他戸親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Osabe|1=Osabe-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11377635}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|qid=Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} |shrine Rank = Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |founded = {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|qid=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 19 ({{ill|800|en|800|qid=Q23677|lt=800|1=800}}) |main hall = Single-bay Kasuga-zukuri, cypress-bark roof |annual festival = {{ill|October 13|en|October 13|qid=Q2925|lt=October 13|1=October 13}} |other names = "Goryō-san" (ごりょうさん) |cultural property = |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 40|lat_sec = 34.41 |long_deg = 135|long_min = 49|long_sec = 51.17 |map = Nara city |location = {{ill|Yakushidōchō|qid=Q28685937|1=Yakushidōchō}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.676225|N|135.83088055555555|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Inoe-naishinnō|qid=Q7244649|1=Princess Inoe}}; {{ill|Kotoshironushi|qid=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Osabe-shinnō|qid=Q11377635|1=Osabe-shinnō}} }} '''Goryō Shrine''' (御霊神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yakushidō-chō|ja|薬師堂町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushidō-chō|1=Yakushidōchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28685937}}, {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. Under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Rankd shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{sfn|Nara Prefecture History 5: Shrines|p=22}}. It is also known as '''Nanto Goryō Shrine''' or '''Kibi Goryō'''From the {{ill|Nara Bōme Sekkai|ja|奈良坊目拙解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Bōme Sekkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 村井古道|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q96711987}}.. The shrine was founded in {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|qid=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 19 ({{ill|800|en|800|qid=Q23677|lt=800|1=800}}) by order of Emperor Kanmu{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://naramachi-nigiwainoie.jp/wp-content/uploads/2021/04/nanakuchi_vol06.pdf |title=Nara Seven Entrances 6: From Kidera Entrance to the Kofun of Emperor Sōdō via the Goryō Path |publisher=Nara City Traditional Culture Executive Committee |accessdate=2022-10-18}}. It is situated southwest of the site of the Five-Story Pagoda of {{ill|Gangō-ji|en|Gangō-ji|qid=Q1203099|lt=Gangō-ji|1=Gangō-ji}}, enshrining {{ill|Princess Inoe|en|Princess Inoe|qid=Q7244649|lt=Empress Inoue|1=Princess Inoe}}, {{ill|Prince Osabe|ja|他戸親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Osabe|1=Osabe-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11377635}}, and other deities collectively known as the Eight Great Deities of the {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11489354}}. Alongside {{ill|Sōdō Emperor Shrine|ja|崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Emperor Shrine|qq=}} in Nishikichiji-chō, it is considered one of the "Two Major Goryō Shrines of Nanto." == History == During the spread of plagues in the new capital of {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|qid=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō|1=Heian-kyō}}, Emperor Kanmu attributed the outbreaks to {{ill|Onryō|en|Onryō|qid=Q634195|lt=vengeful spirits|1=Onryō}}. He ordered the construction of three shrines in the former capital of {{ill|Heijō-kyō|en|Heijō-kyō|qid=Q862509|lt=Heijō-kyō|1=Heijō-kyō}}: one on the Upper Road for {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|qid=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}} (later {{ill|Sōdō Emperor|ja|崇道天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Emperor|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 早良親王|1=Prince Sawara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1079634}}), one on the Lower Road for {{ill|Prince Osabe|ja|他戸親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Osabe|1=Osabe-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11377635}}, and the current shrine on the Mi11le Road for {{ill|Princess Inoe|en|Princess Inoe|qid=Q7244649|lt=Empress Inoue|1=Princess Inoe}}. In {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|qid=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 19 ({{ill|800|en|800|qid=Q23677|lt=800|1=800}}), the shrine was established after transferring the spirit of Empress Inoue from {{ill|Reianji Temple|ja|霊安寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reianji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 御霊神社 (五條市霊安寺町)|1=Goryō Shrine (Reianji-chō)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30924976}} and {{ill|Goryō Shrine (Reianji-chō)|ja|御霊神社 (五條市霊安寺町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō Shrine (Reianji-chō)|qq=}} in Yamato Province (modern {{ill|Gojō, Nara|en|Gojō, Nara|qid=Q602571|lt=Gojō|1=Gojō, Nara}}). It served as the site of {{ill|Goryō-e|ja|御霊会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryō-e|1=goryō-e|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11489354}}. Originally located in front of the South Gate of Gango-ji, the area was known as {{ill|Inoue-chō|ja|井上町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue-chō|1=Inoue-chō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16277538}}. Ancient maps depict a portable shrine hall near the South Gate. After a fire during a peasant uprising in the {{ill|Public security|en|Public security|qid=Q294240|lt=Chian|1=Public security}} era (1022), the shrine was relocated to its current site. As the guardian shrine of Gango-ji, it was later referred to as "Gango-ji Goryō Shrine." During the {{ill|Civilization|en|Civilization|qid=Q8432|lt=Bunmei|1=Civilization}} era, documents describe it as one of the Eight Great Goryō shrines. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine was designated as a village shrine and later became a {{ill|Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen Heihaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. == Worship == [[File:20160802 Goryo-jinja.jpg|thumb|240px|Torii Gate]] In ancient times, portable shrines were placed along the three roads to prevent the spread of plagues. Today, the shrine has an extensive parish area encompassing 70 districts with over 5,000 households. It is widely venerated as a deity of health, prosperity, and peace. The annual festival features a procession that covers all 70 districts over two years. According to the {{ill|Nara Bōme Sekkai|ja|奈良坊目拙解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Bōme Sekkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 村井古道|1=Murai Kodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q96711987}}, the parish boundaries extend from Mochiidono-chō in the north to Kyōbate-mura in the south, and from Kasasagi-chō and Tsurufukuin-chō in the east to Ōmori-mura and Sugiga-chō in the west. == Enshrined Deities == === Main Hall === [[File:Goryō Shrine (Nara) Honden 2.jpg|thumb|150px|Main Hall]] * Main deities: {{ill|Princess Inoe|en|Princess Inoe|qid=Q7244649|lt=Empress Inoue|1=Princess Inoe}}, {{ill|Prince Osabe|ja|他戸親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Osabe|1=Osabe-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11377635}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|qid=Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} === West Hall === * Deities: {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}, {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|en|Tachibana no Hayanari|qid=Q1062686|lt=Tachibana no Hayanari|1=Tachibana no Hayanari}}, {{ill|Fumihara no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumihara no Miyatamaro|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11499944}} === East Hall === * Deities: {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|qid=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|ja|藤原広嗣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|1=Fujiwara no Hirotsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3090626}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623103}} == Grounds == * Main Hall * West Hall * East Hall * Worship Hall [[File:20160805 Goryo-jinja.jpg|thumb|100px|Harai-do Shrine]] * {{ill|Haraedo Shrine|ja|祓戸社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Deities: {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|ja|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi no Kami|lt=Ibukidonushi|ja|気吹戸主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibukidonushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Hayasasunohime|ja|速佐須良比咩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasasunohime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|lt=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} [[File:20160804 Goryo-jinja.jpg|thumb|100px|Shusse Inari Shrine]] * {{ill|Shusse Inari Shrine|ja|出世稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusse Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Saritahiko|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saritahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天鈿女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノウズメ}}, {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|qid=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}}. Established in 1952 after being transferred from {{ill|Shusse Inari Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|出世稲荷神社 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shusse Inari Shrine (Kyoto)|qq=}}. * {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine|ja|若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine|qq=}} - deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}. Relocated in 1877 from Kitamuro-chō. * Mizuhiruko Shrine - deity: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|蛭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヒルコ|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1067028}} * Shrine Office * Main Gate == External Related Shrines == * {{ill|Chintaku Reifu Shrine|ja|鎮宅霊符神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chintaku Reifu Shrine|qq=}} - Located in In'yō-chō. == Treasures == * A pair of six-panel folding screens with portraits of the {{ill|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|en|Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry|qid=Q671039|lt=Thirty-Six Immortal Poets|1=Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == {{osm box|w|314212484}} {{Commonscat|Goryō Shrine (Nara)}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara Prefecture History 5: Shrines|editor=Ikeda Genta, Miyazaka Toshikazu, Nara Prefecture History Editorial Committee|publisher={{ill|Meicho Publishing|ja|名著出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meicho Publishing|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|year=1989|isbn=462601335X|page=265|ref={{sfnref|Nara Prefecture History 5: Shrines}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume|editor=Nara City History Editorial Council|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346755}}|year=1985|page=83|ref={{sfnref|Nara City History: Shrines and Temples Volume}}}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Japanese Historical Place name Series: Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|year=1981|page=518}} * {{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Dramatic Nara|editor=Ogura Tsukiko|publisher=Keihan Information and Education Publishing|year=2010|page=136|isbn=978-4-87806-601-6|ref={{sfnref|Dramatic Nara}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Sōdō Emperor Shrine|ja|崇道天皇社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōdō Emperor Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachioji Shrine (Nara)|ja|八王子社 (奈良市納院町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine (Nara)|qq=}} - Said to be a subordinate shrine. * {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|qid=Q3273210|lt=Goryō Belief|1=Goryō}} * {{ill|Naramachi|ja|ならまち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naramachi|1=Naramachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11274524}} {{translated page|ja|御霊神社 (奈良市)|version=98062311|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御霊神社 (奈良市)]] [[ja:御霊神社]] {{Commons category|Goryō Shrine (Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q22118758}} [[Category:Kasuga-Utsushi]] [[Category:Kōfuku-ji]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
